label: Model-based reinforcement learning

node.label: Model-based reinforcement learning
node.definition: The agent learns or uses a predictive environment model to evaluate and plan future actions.
node.why_it_matters: The role of Model-based reinforcement learning is to connect Reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state to downstream capabilities including Latent world models & predictive dynamics, World-model rollout & imagination, Game-playing, control & simulation agents.
examples: substrate for Latent world models & predictive dynamics
substrate for Game-playing, control & simulation agents
limitations: The chosen learning signal and interaction pattern may not match the deployment objective or data-generating process. Its direct dependencies include Reinforcement learning, Latent variables & hidden state.

dependencies.rationale: Model-based reinforcement learning follows the learning setup expressed by Reinforcement learning.
Model-based reinforcement learning uses Latent variables & hidden state as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Model-based reinforcement learning uses Multimodal, simulated & streaming data as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
Model-based reinforcement learning uses Dynamical systems & control theory as a reusable ingredient in its defined operation.
